title: Make journal badges accessible — convert span to button with keyboard/focus support
analysis: The journal entry count badge on line 153 of recon.html is a with @click="open = !open" — this is inaccessible because screen readers don't announce spans as interactive elements, and spans are not keyboard-focusable by default. The fix converts it to a

steps.description: Write failing test: journal badge renders as <button> element, not <span>. Add test_badge_is_button_element to TestReconJournalBadge asserting '<button' and '</button>' in rendered HTML, and '<span' not wrapping the badge (use data-testid to isolate).
Write failing test: journal badge has aria-label="Show 3 journal entries" (using MOCK_RUNS journal_entry_count=3). Add test_badge_has_aria_label to TestReconJournalBadge.
Write failing test: journal badge has hover:bg-blue-800 class for visual hover feedback. Add test_badge_has_hover_style to TestReconJournalBadge.
Write failing test: journal badge has focus:ring styles (focus:ring-2, focus:ring-blue-400). Add test_badge_has_focus_ring_styles to TestReconJournalBadge.
Write failing test: journal badge does NOT have cursor-pointer class (redundant on native button elements). Add test_badge_no_cursor_pointer to TestReconJournalBadge.
Write failing test: aria-label uses dynamic count — test with journal_entry_count=5 to verify "Show 5 journal entries" appears. Add test_badge_aria_label_dynamic_count to TestReconJournalBadge.
Update recon.html line 153: change to .
Update existing test_badge_shown_when_count_positive: replace '3' alternative in the assertion with '3' to match the new element type.

design_decisions.decision: Use native <button> element instead of span+role="button"+tabindex
design_decisions.rationale: Native buttons provide keyboard focusability, Enter/Space activation, and screen reader announcement for free — no ARIA workarounds needed. This follows the first rule of ARIA: use native HTML elements when possible.
The dashboard uses a dark theme (bg-gray-800 cards). Ring offset must match the card background for the focus ring to be visible and look correct against the dark surface.
Native <button> elements already show a pointer cursor in browsers. Keeping cursor-pointer is redundant and masks the semantic improvement.
Screen reader users need to know what clicking the button does AND how many entries will be shown. 'Show 3 journal entries' is more informative than a generic 'Toggle journal entries' label.

reuse.how: All new tests reuse the existing _patch_recon_data() context manager and MOCK_RUNS (which has journal_entry_count=3). For dynamic count test, override runs with a modified copy.
Add new test methods to the existing class rather than creating a new one. The class already tests journal badge presence/absence.
Follow the same pattern of <button @click="..."> already used for the Show/Hide tasks toggle, ensuring consistency across dashboard templates.
